markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Floating Tent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2580)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This diamond-shaped tent is designed for sleeping in planar environments without gravity, such as the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4). Weights are attached at each of its six points, carefully balanced against each other to prevent the tent from leaning too far in any one direction. The tent has an anchor, which can be used to moor the tent and prevent it from floating away from the object or location it is anchored to.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Floating Tent (Pup)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

A floating pup tent is large enough for one person and their supplies. It contains one hammock suspended in the center of the tent, large enough for a Medium or smaller creature to comfortably sleep, in addition to a net beneath the hammock where gear can be stored.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Floating Tent (Four-Person)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

A standard floating tent is large enough for four creatures and their supplies, containing four hammocks in two rows suspended in the center of the tent and large nets near the top and bottom of the tent for gear.
name: Floating Tent (Four-Person)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Frost Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2581)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Bottled Breath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=499" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bottle of frozen vapors is captured in the frozen peaks of the crown of the world. After inhaling frost breath, you gain resistance 5 to cold. You can exhale the frost breath as a single action to release a spray of frigid air in a 15- foot cone. Each creature in the area takes 4d6 cold damage with a DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. For 10 minutes, surfaces in the area are covered in ice, becoming difficult terrain and uneven ground ([Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) DC 20).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Frost Breath

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jaathoom's Scarf](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2582)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This scarf is made of fine silk that's the same shade of blue as a clear, cloudless sky. The short ends are edged with a fine gold fringe that seems to sway even in still weather as though touched by invisible winds. The long edges have exquisite embroidery in threads that vary from a blue identical to the silk to the deep gray of storm clouds during winter. Wearing this scarf grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks to dance and to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79).

**Activate—Vanish** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The scarf casts 4th-rank [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) on you.

**Activate—Jaathoom's Rebuke** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You let the winds around you catch the edges of the _jaathoom's scarf_, and a jaathoom shuyookh appears with a sudden updraft. The winds force your enemies back, granting you some breathing room in battle. Each enemy in a 10-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 27 Fortitude save or be [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet. A creature that critically fails is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) after being moved. Creatures with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait are immune to all these effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Jaathoom's Scarf

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Nimbus Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2583)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Bottled Breath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=499" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 75

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) of Cloudspire Citadel in Golarion's Mwangi Expanse create bottles of _nimbus breath_ by capturing and bottling the fluffiest nimbus clouds from the lairs of cloud dragons. After harvesting the cloud, the bottle must sit at the peak of the tallest mountain for 10 days and 10 nights, pelted by all forms of weather. _Nimbus breaths_ can also be created by capturing clouds on other planes, such as those found on the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4) or in the highest peaks of Celestia.

 After you inhale the _nimbus breath_, you gain resistance 5 to electricity and are affected by [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=125) as long as you hold your breath.  You can exhale the breath as a single action to create a [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (DC 24) from your mouth. If you're airborne when the cloud expires, you float gently to the ground at 60 feet per round and don't take damage from this fall.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Nimbus Breath

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76 **Price** 2,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- _Spiral chimes_ are a set of small metal bells decorated with spiraling designs that hang on thin wires from a carved statue of a storm cloud or air elemental. Each bell in the spiral chimes rings at a different tone, and when caught in the turbulent winds of a storm, the bells combine to create deep, full songs. A set of spiral chimes is a planar key for [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) and similar magic. When the chimes are used this way, you're more likely to arrive where you intend to be, appearing 1d6×25 miles from your intended destination instead of 1d10×25 miles away. **Activate—Forecast Chime** 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=132)) **Effect** You let the _spiral chimes_ play in the wind, interpreting the music to predict the weather at your location for the next 24 hours. You receive advanced warning of the temperature, humidity, storms, natural disasters (such as tornadoes, floods, or volcanic eruptions), and other weather conditions that naturally arise. The information you gain through the _spiral chimes_ doesn't take magical occurrences that might change the weather into account. **Activate—Revealing Chime** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You ring the chimes, blanketing everything in a 30-foot burst within 120 feet in visible, reverberating sound. This can negate invisibility, making creatures [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) instead of [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26). The duration and other effects depend on the result of each creature's attempt at a DC 30 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target's invisibility is negated for 2 rounds.
**Failure** The target is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 minute, and its invisibility is negated for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The target is deafened for 10 minutes, and its invisibility is negated for 10 minutes.
name: Spiral Chimes

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spun Cloud](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2585)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Bottled Breath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=499" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

On the Elemental [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4), small clouds of differing colors can sometimes float separately from other cloud formations. Clever people can spin these clouds into small handfuls, creating a magical bundle of elemental power. Unlike most bottled breath, spun clouds don't have any effect on you while you hold them in your lungs, but you can exhale the cloud as a single action. When exhaled, the cloud flows out and expands into a 20-foot burst within 60 feet of you. The cloud dissipates after 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spun Cloud (Black)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A dark storm cloud conceals creatures within it. Creatures outside the cloud are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to creatures within it.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spun Cloud (Blue)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A blue cloud protects against lightning. Creatures gain resistance 5 to electricity while in the cloud.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spun Cloud (Green)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A green cloud smells terrible, making those inside ill. Any creature that ends its turn in the cloud must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or be [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (or sickened 2 on a critical failure). Regardless of the result of its save, the creature becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours. This is an [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) effect.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spun Cloud (Red)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A red cloud terrifies those it surrounds. Any creature in the cloud, or that later enters the cloud, must succeed at a DC 24 Will save or become [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (or frightened 3 on a critical failure). Regardless of the result of its save, the creature becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours. This is an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spun Cloud (White)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A white cloud swirls with winds that speed up travel. A creature that starts its turn in the cloud gains a +10-foot item bonus to its land Speed and fly Speed (if it has one) until the end of its turn.
name: Spun Cloud

On the Elemental [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4), small clouds of differing colors can sometimes float separately from other cloud formations. Clever people can spin these clouds into small handfuls, creating a magical bundle of elemental power. Unlike most bottled breath, spun clouds don't have any effect on you while you hold them in your lungs, but you can exhale the cloud as a single action. When exhaled, the cloud flows out and expands into a 20-foot burst within 60 feet of you. The cloud dissipates after 1 minute.

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Bottled Breath" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=499" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Storm breaths_ are bottles of temperamental lightning captured during storms on the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4), releasing small charges of static energy any time they're touched. The first _storm breaths_ were created by [Ranginori's](/Deities.aspx?ID=106) faithful following the Elemental Lord's return from a long imprisonment, but the recipe has since been duplicated across the multiverse. After inhaling _storm breath_, you gain resistance 5 to both electricity and sonic. You can exhale the storm breath as a bolt of lightning, dealing 4d12 electricity damage to all creatures in a 30-foot line, with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Storm Breath

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wisp Chain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2587)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)_ is made of small, interlocking currents of wind carefully woven together. While the links of air don't jingle against each other like chain links might, the tiny cyclones nevertheless create a blustering howl akin to standing at the center of a storm. A creature that ends its turn adjacent to you must attempt a DC 23 Fortitude save. On a failure, it becomes [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) until it moves away from you.

**Activate—Slicing Links** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You unbind the currents that form your armor and release them as cutting whorls of air that slice into creatures in a 60-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 6d6 slashing damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet (or 20 feet on a critical failure). You lose the AC bonus of your armor until the end of your turn, when the air currents reform the wisp chain around you.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wisp Chain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2587)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)_ is made of small, interlocking currents of wind carefully woven together. While the links of air don't jingle against each other like chain links might, the tiny cyclones nevertheless create a blustering howl akin to standing at the center of a storm. A creature that ends its turn adjacent to you must attempt a DC 23 Fortitude save. On a failure, it becomes [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) until it moves away from you.

**Activate—Slicing Links** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You unbind the currents that form your armor and release them as cutting whorls of air that slice into creatures in a 60-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 6d6 slashing damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save is also [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet (or 20 feet on a critical failure). You lose the AC bonus of your armor until the end of your turn, when the air currents reform the wisp chain around you.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Drought Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2588)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A gray powder that smells like wet rock, _drought powder_ has various uses against water. If you sprinkle it over your body, you and items you carry or wear remain dry for the next 24 hours. Thrown into a body of water, the dust lowers the water in an area 50 feet long by 50 feet wide by 10 feet. You can fling it in the air, coating all creatures in a 10-foot burst centered on a point within 5 feet of you. Creatures who have the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait in that area are affected as if by a [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) spell (DC 28). If you attempt to use the dust in other, similar ways, the GM decides whether the dust can accomplish your aims.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Drought Powder

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Exuviae Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2589)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Exuviae powder comes from cast-off shells of cicada-like insects native to the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5), crushed to dust. You sprinkle this powder over your body, leaving an iridescent layer reminiscent of insect chitin. After you do so, for 8 hours, you double the time you can [hold your breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). During this time, you also have access to the shed chitin activation.

**Activate—Shed Chitin** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))  **Trigger** You would be [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30);  **Effect** The powder petrifies like a shell around you instead, and its other effects end. The powder causes you to become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 minute as well as [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 25). You can use the extra action each round only for Escape and Stride actions.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Exuviae Powder

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fossil Fragment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2590)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A fossil fragment is a piece of a fossil creature, typically a smaller bone from a larger specimen.

**Activate—Fossile Metamorphosis** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You activate the fragment by placing it on solid ground and then speaking its name, causing the fragment to form the full fossilized skeleton of a creature. In creature form, the fragment has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) trait. Because it's an animated fossil instead of a living creature, it has the [construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=35) and [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55) traits and lacks its normal creature type trait (typically [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9)). It's also immune to bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510), and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38). It can understand your language, and it obeys you to the best of its ability when you use an action to command it. The specifics of each creature, as well as the activation's frequency, if any, appear in its entry below.

 If the fragment is slain while in creature form, it reverts to its fragment shape and can't be activated again for 1 week. If the fragment is destroyed, its magic is lost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Amber Mosquito)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A minuscule insect preserved in fossilized tree sap, this fragment becomes a [giant mosquito](/Monsters.aspx?ID=733) when activated. It can be called upon once per day for up to 10 minutes. The fossil mosquito can't afflict anyone with septic malaria. If the mosquito uses Blood Drain, it doesn't gain temporary Hit Points, but instead collects blood from the victim. The blood stays within the mosquito indefinitely and stays fresh while it does. If the mosquito uses Blood Drain again, any blood from before that use is lost.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Brontosaurus Phalange)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 21,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This massive toe bone becomes a [brontosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=122) when activated. It can be called upon no more than four times per month. The skeleton remains for 24 hours if used as a beast of burden or for transport. If it attempts an attack or otherwise engage in combat, it reverts to its fragment form after 1d4 rounds. The skeleton is so massive and sturdy that it can serve as the base of a structure (from an item or spell effect with the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait), provided the structure is no larger than 20 feet in width or height. When the brontosaurus reverts to its fragment form, the structure reverts with it.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Deinonychus Claw)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This curved claw becomes a [deinonychus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=118) when activated. It can be called upon once per day and can remain in deinonychus form for no more than 10 minutes. The skeleton can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Eurypterid Paddle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The tip of an oar-like limb specialized for swimming becomes a [spiny eurypterid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1152) when activated. It can be called upon once a week for up to 24 hours. The eurypterid can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller, and when it does, it confers the ability to breathe both air and water upon its rider.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Petrified Wood)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 4,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This colorful sliver of petrified wood becomes an [awakened tree](/Monsters.aspx?ID=28) when activated. It doesn't have the normal weaknesses of an awakened tree, but it's rooted in place, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). As a single action, it can throw a petrified seed, such as a stone pine cone or acorn, up to 60 feet. A copy of the tree appears there, provided there's an unoccupied space large enough for it. While two trees exist, if either tree throws another seed, one of the existing trees disappears, replaced by the new tree. The tree can be called upon once per day for up to 1 minute. This duration starts when you activate the item, and all trees disappear when it ends.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Triceratops Frill)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This small piece of triceratops frill turns into a [triceratops](/Monsters.aspx?ID=121) when activated. It can be called upon once per day and can remain in triceratops form for no more than 10 minutes. The skeleton can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Tyrannosaur Tooth)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This dagger-shaped tooth turns into a [tyrannosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=123) when activated. It can be called upon once per day and can remain in tyrannosaurus form for no more than 10 minutes. The skeleton can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller.

 If the fragment is slain while in creature form, it reverts to its fragment shape and can't be activated again for 1 week. If the fragment is destroyed, its magic is lost.

It can understand your language, and it obeys you to the best of its ability when you use an action to command it. The specifics of each creature, as well as the activation's frequency, if any, appear in its entry below.

 If the fragment is slain while in creature form, it reverts to its fragment shape and can't be activated again for 1 week. If the fragment is destroyed, its magic is lost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Amber Mosquito)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A minuscule insect preserved in fossilized tree sap, this fragment becomes a [giant mosquito](/Monsters.aspx?ID=733) when activated. It can be called upon once per day for up to 10 minutes. The fossil mosquito can't afflict anyone with septic malaria. If the mosquito uses Blood Drain, it doesn't gain temporary Hit Points, but instead collects blood from the victim. The blood stays within the mosquito indefinitely and stays fresh while it does. If the mosquito uses Blood Drain again, any blood from before that use is lost.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Brontosaurus Phalange)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 21,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This massive toe bone becomes a [brontosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=122) when activated. It can be called upon no more than four times per month. The skeleton remains for 24 hours if used as a beast of burden or for transport. If it attempts an attack or otherwise engage in combat, it reverts to its fragment form after 1d4 rounds. The skeleton is so massive and sturdy that it can serve as the base of a structure (from an item or spell effect with the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait), provided the structure is no larger than 20 feet in width or height. When the brontosaurus reverts to its fragment form, the structure reverts with it.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Deinonychus Claw)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This curved claw becomes a [deinonychus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=118) when activated. It can be called upon once per day and can remain in deinonychus form for no more than 10 minutes. The skeleton can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Eurypterid Paddle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The tip of an oar-like limb specialized for swimming becomes a [spiny eurypterid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1152) when activated. It can be called upon once a week for up to 24 hours. The eurypterid can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller, and when it does, it confers the ability to breathe both air and water upon its rider.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Petrified Wood)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 4,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This colorful sliver of petrified wood becomes an [awakened tree](/Monsters.aspx?ID=28) when activated. It doesn't have the normal weaknesses of an awakened tree, but it's rooted in place, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). As a single action, it can throw a petrified seed, such as a stone pine cone or acorn, up to 60 feet. A copy of the tree appears there, provided there's an unoccupied space large enough for it. While two trees exist, if either tree throws another seed, one of the existing trees disappears, replaced by the new tree. The tree can be called upon once per day for up to 1 minute. This duration starts when you activate the item, and all trees disappear when it ends.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Triceratops Frill)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This small piece of triceratops frill turns into a [triceratops](/Monsters.aspx?ID=121) when activated. It can be called upon once per day and can remain in triceratops form for no more than 10 minutes. The skeleton can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Tyrannosaur Tooth)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This dagger-shaped tooth turns into a [tyrannosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=123) when activated. It can be called upon once per day and can remain in tyrannosaurus form for no more than 10 minutes. The skeleton can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller.

It's also immune to bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510), and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38). It can understand your language, and it obeys you to the best of its ability when you use an action to command it. The specifics of each creature, as well as the activation's frequency, if any, appear in its entry below.

 If the fragment is slain while in creature form, it reverts to its fragment shape and can't be activated again for 1 week. If the fragment is destroyed, its magic is lost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Amber Mosquito)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A minuscule insect preserved in fossilized tree sap, this fragment becomes a [giant mosquito](/Monsters.aspx?ID=733) when activated. It can be called upon once per day for up to 10 minutes. The fossil mosquito can't afflict anyone with septic malaria. If the mosquito uses Blood Drain, it doesn't gain temporary Hit Points, but instead collects blood from the victim. The blood stays within the mosquito indefinitely and stays fresh while it does. If the mosquito uses Blood Drain again, any blood from before that use is lost.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Brontosaurus Phalange)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 21,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This massive toe bone becomes a [brontosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=122) when activated. It can be called upon no more than four times per month. The skeleton remains for 24 hours if used as a beast of burden or for transport. If it attempts an attack or otherwise engage in combat, it reverts to its fragment form after 1d4 rounds. The skeleton is so massive and sturdy that it can serve as the base of a structure (from an item or spell effect with the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait), provided the structure is no larger than 20 feet in width or height. When the brontosaurus reverts to its fragment form, the structure reverts with it.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Deinonychus Claw)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This curved claw becomes a [deinonychus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=118) when activated. It can be called upon once per day and can remain in deinonychus form for no more than 10 minutes. The skeleton can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Eurypterid Paddle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The tip of an oar-like limb specialized for swimming becomes a [spiny eurypterid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1152) when activated. It can be called upon once a week for up to 24 hours. The eurypterid can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller, and when it does, it confers the ability to breathe both air and water upon its rider.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Fossil Fragment (Petrified Wood)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98

**Price** 4,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This colorful sliver of petrified wood becomes an [awakened tree](/Monsters.aspx?ID=28) when activated. It doesn't have the normal weaknesses of an awakened tree, but it's rooted in place, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). As a single action, it can throw a petrified seed, such as a stone pine cone or acorn, up to 60 feet. A copy of the tree appears there, provided there's an unoccupied space large enough for it. While two trees exist, if either tree throws another seed, one of the existing trees disappears, replaced by the new tree. The tree can be called upon once per day for up to 1 minute. This duration starts when you activate the item, and all trees disappear when it ends.

</column>

---

This small piece of triceratops frill turns into a [triceratops](/Monsters.aspx?ID=121) when activated. It can be called upon once per day and can remain in triceratops form for no more than 10 minutes. The skeleton can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller.

Fossil Fragment (Tyrannosaur Tooth)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This dagger-shaped tooth turns into a [tyrannosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=123) when activated. It can be called upon once per day and can remain in tyrannosaurus form for no more than 10 minutes. The skeleton can serve as a mount for a creature one size smaller than it or smaller.

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 99 **Price** 1,800 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand --- _Jabali's dice_ are two six-sided dice carved from evenly weighted stone to the specifications of a specific jabali shuyookh. The sides showing a 6 also have the name and title of the shuyookh inscribed in Petran. If you whisper the name and title during a dice game using _jabali's dice_, they bless you with a bit of luck, granting a +2 item bonus to your [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check. You can do so frequently enough to apply this bonus while [Earning Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23) using Games Lore, but only one user at a time can do so. **Activate—Jabali's Gamble** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You call out the shuyookh's name and title, then roll the dice. The shuyookh appears briefly to provide for your defense. Roll 2d6 to determine the effect. Represented by the GM, the shuyookh chooses any effect's specifications, benefiting you according to the shuyookh's whims.
**2–5**The shuyookh casts [_mountain resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=312) on you.
**6–9**The shuyookh casts [_grasping earth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1334), but you and your allies are immune to it.
**10–11**The shuyookh casts [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=365). The edges can pass through creatures, which are shunted to the side of the wall opposite that edge.
**12**The shuyookh casts a DC 31 [_petrify_](/Spells.aspx?ID=123) spell on a creature hostile and in proximity to you. If no appealing target exists, you receive a 10–11 result instead.
name: Jabali's Dice

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Limestone Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2592)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 99

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Tower Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=4) is a slab of limestone, shaved to a portable size and weight. The shield has Hardness 7 and 28 Hit Points.

**Activate—Limestone Wall** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You set the shield down as it expands up to 60 feet wide and 10 feet tall. Each square of the wall has AC 10, Hardness 7, and 28 Hit Points, and it's immune to critical hits and precision damage. If a section is destroyed, this effect ends, and your limestone shield is broken. You can Dismiss this activation, which otherwise lasts for 1 minute.

**Activate—Block Elements** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're targeted by an effect with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait;  **Effect** The shield expands to block the triggering effect, granting you [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) from that effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Limestone Shield

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Robe of Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2593)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 99

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A robe of stone, decorated with patterns reminiscent of gems and geodes, constantly sheds tiny amounts of dust. While wearing it, you gain [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) in a radius of 10 feet, and you can speak and read [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109). Also, you can eat earth (soil, sand), gems (crystals, glass), and metal instead of food to meet your daily nutrition requirements. You find eating these materials as pleasant as food—the more valuable, the more delicious.

**Activate—Become Stone** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The cloak casts [_elemental form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=98) on you, transforming you into an earth elemental. In addition to the spell's normal effects, you can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93) through any earthen matter, including rock, moving at the spell's burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of your passing. Also, the range of the tremorsense you gain from the robe increases to 30 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Robe of Stone

[Sairazul Blue](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2594)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 100

**Price** 180 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _Sairazul blue potion_ is a rich navy blue in color. Subjects of the [Crystalline Queen](/Deities.aspx?ID=107) produced the potion to protect themselves from the radiation [Ayrzul](/Deities.aspx?ID=102) left behind. For the next 8 hours, your skin becomes navy blue, and you gain resistance 5 to poison damage and void damage. If you drop to 0 Hit Points due to poison or void damage, the Sairazul blue within your body reacts, healing you for 8d8 Hit Points. The resistances the potion grants then end.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sairazul Blue

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sandcastle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2595)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 100

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L inactivated
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _sandcastle_ comes in a cylindrical, waterproof satchel that contains densely packed fine sand. The sand's color depends on where it was collected when the item was made. This sand can be shaped and magically activated to create a fortification made of hard-packed sand. It can survive most ordinary weather, but a _sandcastle_ has little resilience against water. It collapses if activated on water or if the structure is caught in a deluge or heavy rainfall. Each 10-foot-by-10-foot section of the wall has AC 10, Hardness 10, and 40 Hit Points. It's immune to critical hits and precision damage, and it has weakness to water 15. If destroyed, a section of the _sandcastle_ becomes a pile of sand, or wet sand if it was destroyed by water. This sand is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453), which lasts for the remaining duration of the activation. It's easy to make handholds and footholds in the sand walls, so the DC to [climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) the walls is 15.

**Activate—Shape the Castle** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You empty the satchel and shape the sand into a miniature castle. When finished, you utter “build” in [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), and the _sandcastle_ swiftly builds into a full-size structure. The castle is shaped as you choose, up to 120 feet in any dimension. Its walls must be 10 feet thick, and each story must be at least 10 feet tall from floor to ceiling. The _sandcastle_ has only rudimentary furnishings with no moving parts—even a chair or door is too complicated, but block-like benches and platforms can be created. Typically, the castle has little more than staircases and windows.

 You can return the _sandcastle_ to its portable form by using one Interact action to fill the item's satchel with sand from the castle.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sandcastle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Singing Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2596)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 100

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _singing stone_ looks like a drinking cup made of polished rock, and it always faintly hums or keens. Anyone who carries the cup for some time senses that it changes its tune depending on the types of rock nearby and that it grows quiet in areas with little stone. A _singing stone_ is a planar key for [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) and similar magic. When it's used this way, you're more likely to arrive where you intend to be, appearing 1d6×25 miles from your intended destination instead of 1d10×25 miles away.

**Activate—Stone's Speech** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _singing stone_ casts [_speak with stones_](/Spells.aspx?ID=310), allowing you to speak and listen through the bowl to communicate with stones. You can use the spell normally, or, as you activate the _singing stone_, you can target one stone you can clearly identify in appearance and location. This target must be on the same plane as you or on the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5). You can't change targets during a single activation.

**Activate—Stone's Sight** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=138)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Placing the _singing stone_ against a rocky surface, you cause it to reverberate, revealing what's behind or beneath the surface. You get a mental image of this area that's 15 feet deep and 5 feet in diameter. The image doesn't convey color, but it's clear to you what objects or creatures within are moving and which are stationary. The image is instant, however, and therefore doesn't allow you to track movement over time.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Singing Stone

---

You can throw a _stalagmite seed_, use it as a sling stone, or pack it into firearm ammunition. When you use the seed, you aim it at a 5-foot square rather than a specific target. Stalagmites of assorted sizes erupt in the square the seed lands in, dealing 6d6 piercing damage to any creature within that space (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The stalagmites remain for 1 minute, creating [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) in that space, before they crumble into dust.

</column>

Life-infused soil from the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5), _vital earth_ is glittering dust you inhale that causes you to not need air or water for 24 hours. Also, for this time, your wounds close easily, like molding clay, meaning someone attempting to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54) or [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57) on you doesn't need a [healer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25) and gains a +1 item bonus to the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ash Gown](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2599)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 122

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn clothing

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Ash gowns_ are formal wear spun from smoke, ash, and char collected from the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). Typically, they're voluminous floor-length dresses or three-piece suits, but regardless of their specific tailoring, _ash gowns_ are always an ostentatious display of wealth and loyalty to the powers of the Plane of Fire. They're exceptionally popular in the courts of the [Elemental Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=8) and among the high society of Medina Mudii'a. The gown grants you resistance 5 to fire and a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks.

**Activate—Blazing Promenade** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The ash gown ignites in a ferocious blaze, flames licking the floor and trailing behind you like a dancing cape. You Stride and make a Strike at the end of your movement. During the Stride, your flames incinerate minor obstacles in your path; you ignore non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453), and any you move through is destroyed. Creatures that are adjacent to you at any point during your movement take 2d6 fire damage with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature doesn't need to attempt this save more than once, even if you move past it multiple times.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The resistance to fire is 10, and the bonus to Intimidation checks is +2. You can activate a _greater ash gown_ once per 10 minutes instead of once per day. The activation's damage increases to 4d6, and its DC increases to 28.

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 122 **Price** 2,750 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- The exterior of this egg-shaped brass censer is polished to a mirror-like sheen. Several rings are attached to its sides at regular intervals. The top of the censer's lid is decorated with a pair of intertwining snakes. **Activate—Light Incense** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))**Cost** incense worth at least 5 gp; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** A piping, reddish smoke pours from the censer in a 20-foot emanation. You choose whether the smoke causes the target's blood to turn extremely hot or transmutes to poison; the smoke deals your choice of fire or poison damage. Each living creature that's in the area or enters it attempts a DC 34 Fortitude saving throw, then becomes temporarily immune for 1 hour. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature breathes in a small amount of the poisonous smoke and takes 2d6 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Failure** The creature gulps down a lungful of the smoke, taking 4d6 persistent damage and becoming [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) until the persistent damage ends.
**Critical Failure** The creature inhales a large amount of the smoke, taking 6d6 persistent damage and becoming enfeebled 3 until the persistent damage ends.
name: Bloodburn Censer

>
[Brazier of Harmony](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2601)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Censer" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=500" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 122

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _brazier of harmony_ is a circular, orb-shaped censer etched with celebrating creatures shaking hands and dancing. The brazier contains a pleasant-smelling potpourri of dried flowers and incenses, designed to create a calm atmosphere that encourages meditation, thoughtfulness, and camaraderie. While holding the lit censer, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks, whether the censer is activated or not.

**Activate—Light Incense** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))**Cost** incense worth at least 1 sp; **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** When the incense is lit, pleasant, floral smoke surrounds the censer in a 20-foot emanation, creating a space of peace and harmony. Each creature that breathes the smoke is affected by 3rd-rank [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=31) and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours. The spell's effects end when the incense burns out.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Brazier of Harmony

    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Candle of Inflamed Passions](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2602)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 122

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This blood-red candle is made from wax derived from oil shale found in certain parts of the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). The wick burns with a flame that flickers and dances even if there's no draft to stir it. You activate the candle by lighting it, which causes creatures within 10 feet of the candle to find their emotions running high. Creatures in the area take a –1 status penalty to saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects. Once lit, the candle burns for 10 minutes. If extinguished, it can't be relit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Candle of Inflamed Passions

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 122 **Price** 1,750 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand --- This lump of coal is always warm to the touch and glows faintly red from within, as if holding an ember of flame waiting to be stoked. No amount of water or coldness can extinguish the coal's warmth. When you hold an _everburning coal_ in your hand, you gain resistance 10 to cold and are protected from [mild, severe, and extreme cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642). In addition, an _everburning coal_ is a planar key for [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) and similar magic to travel to the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). When using it this way, you can attune it to the fires of your destination to make it more likely to arrive where you intend to be, appearing 1d6 × 25 miles from your intended destination instead of 1d10 × 25 miles away. **Activate—Coal Wall** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _everburning coal_ creates a towering wall of hot coals. This has the effect of [_wall of ice_](/Spells.aspx?ID=364), except for the following adjustments.
  • The wall has the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) trait instead of [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) and [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165).
  • The wall deals fire damage instead of cold damage.
  • The weakness to fire is instead weakness to cold and to water.
  • Destroying a section of the wall with cold or water (rather than with fire) causes a section to evaporate.
name: Everburning Coal

    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Globe of Shrouds](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2604)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Censer" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=500" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Revelation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=138" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 123

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The body of this censer is made of transparent crystal banded with dark iron. This globe hangs from a sturdy chain attached to a simple steel rod with a smooth grip.

**Activate—Light Incense** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))**Cost** incense worth at least 5 gp; **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** As you light the incense, barely visible smoke issues from the censer in a hazy 20-foot emanation. Creatures that are in the haze or later enter it are wreathed in wisps of smoke; these wisps last while the creature is in the smoke's aura and until the start of its next turn if it leaves the haze. An ally in the aura is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) and gains a +2 status bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks. Any enemy in the aura that is or becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) appears as a translucent shape to you and your allies—it's no longer [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), but it remains concealed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Globe of Shrouds

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lambent Perfume](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2605)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Censer" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=500" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 123

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ceramic incense burner is shaped like a three-headed ibis in the form of [Atreia](/Deities.aspx?ID=101), spreading his wings and preparing to take flight; it hangs on a thin, golden chain.

**Activate—Light Incense** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))**Cost** incense worth at least 1 sp; **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Sparkling golden mist escapes the ibis's open beaks, spreading in a 20-foot emanation. This perfume mist is calming and restorative. A creature that ends its turn within the censer's smoke while [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) or under an affliction can attempt a new saving throw to overcome it. A creature with multiple afflictions, or that is both sickened and has an affliction, chooses one to attempt to overcome each time it ends its turn in the aura. After attempting a new saving throw against an affliction, a creature is temporarily immune to _lambent perfume_ for the purpose of overcoming that affliction for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lambent Perfume

This black obsidian blade is a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [gun sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=218)_. Magma seeps along its many cracks and crags, and the handle is hot but not scalding to the touch. Strikes with this gun sword deal 1 extra fire damage.

**Activate—Explosion of Shards** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;  **Requirements** Your most recent action this turn was a successful ranged Strike with _obsidian edge_; **Effect** Magma coats the sword blade completely before exploding from your weapon in a 15-foot emanation of fire and glass. Creatures in the area take 2d6 fire damage and 2d6 piercing damage with a DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

**Activate—Explosion of Shards** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;  **Requirements** Your most recent action this turn was a successful ranged Strike with _obsidian edge_; **Effect** Magma coats the sword blade completely before exploding from your weapon in a 15-foot emanation of fire and glass. Creatures in the area take 2d6 fire damage and 2d6 piercing damage with a DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rhyton of the Radiant Ifrit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2607)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 124

**Price** 4,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This exquisite, shimmering rhyton is made from volcanic glass and encrusted with rubies, garnets, and other red gemstones, with a blown glass sculpture of a snakelike fire elemental at its base. It was sculpted as part of a 10-piece set of drinking horns, created to match a powerful ifrit shuyookh's entertaining dinnerware, but it became separated from the rest of the collection. Whether for good or ill, the beautiful glass rhyton still carries tidings from its previous shuyookh owner, and you become their esteemed guest from afar whenever you hold it.

 The drinking horn functions as a [bottomless stein](/Equipment.aspx?ID=563), refilling with a common ale each time it's emptied. However, anyone holding the rhyton who whispers the name of its former shuyookh owner has the horn filled with their favorite wine, spirit, juice, or other beverage from anywhere across the many planes, as if their wish were granted by a magnanimous genie.

**Activate—Toast!** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You raise a toast to a creature or creatures you're about to socialize with. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against those creatures for 1 hour.

**Activate—Ifrit's Command** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** An ifrit's hospitality always comes with an implied threat. You cause the shuyookh to briefly appear and take its vengeance on those who would hurt you, the genie's “guest.” The shuyookh issues a 6th-rank [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) that targets all creatures [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23) to you in range instead of the usual number of targets. The shuyookh issues the same command to all of them. Each target that fails its save also feels all nourishment leached from it, becoming [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) as long as it's affected by the _command_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rhyton of the Radiant Ifrit

**Base Weapon** [Spiked Gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Prized by naari geniekin who prefer to fight with their fists, _scalding gauntlets_ are a pair of _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spiked gauntlets](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11)_. The intricate golden gauntlets are engraved with [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) writing praising the glories of the Dominion of Flame and embellished with shimmering black and red gemstones. A creature you [grab](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrain](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) while wearing the gauntlets must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or take 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and be [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) from the pain; it's temporarily immune to being sickened by _scalding gauntlets_ for 1 hour.

The intricate golden gauntlets are engraved with [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) writing praising the glories of the Dominion of Flame and embellished with shimmering black and red gemstones. A creature you [grab](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrain](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) while wearing the gauntlets must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or take 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and be [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) from the pain; it's temporarily immune to being sickened by _scalding gauntlets_ for 1 hour.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Smoke Veil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2609)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 124

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Smoke veils_ are wigs or headdresses made of flame and ash, giving the wearer a burning coil of fiery hair and concealing their face behind a smoldering, omnipresent haze of smoke and sparking embers. You can use the veil to go unrecognized by hiding your face so that you can attempt [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) without needing a [disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19). When you do so, it takes you only 1 minute to create the disguise, and you gain a +1 item bonus to the check. You still need a disguise kit and the full time if you're using cosmetics and other props to change other aspects of your disguise, or if Impersonating a specific person.

**Activate—Blazing Stare** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))   **Requirements** You dealt fire damage to a target you can see within 30 feet with your most recent action this turn; **Effect** You set your fiery gaze on your target, eyes burning within a cloud of ash and cinder. Roll an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) the target. Demoralize loses the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait and gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait, and you don't take a penalty when you attempt to Demoralize a creature that doesn't understand your language.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Smoke Veil

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sparkshade Parasol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2610)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 124

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large magical parasol shields you from the heat in hot environments, no matter whether the heat comes from above, like the beating sun, or below, like roiling lava. While holding the _sparkshade parasol_, you gain resistance 10 to fire and are protected from [mild, severe, and extreme environmental heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=642).

**Activate—Parasol's Protection** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Trigger** You're targeted by or inside the area of an effect that deals fire damage;  **Effect** You hold your parasol between yourself and the incoming flames, gaining fire resistance 20 against the triggering effect. (This applies only to any damage the effect deals.) For the next 1 minute, flames dance harmlessly along the parasol's brim, letting you use Parasol's Pyrotechnics.

**Activate—Parasol's Pyrotechnics** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))   **Requirements** Flames are dancing on the sparkshade parasol due to you using Parasol's Protection; **Effect** You release captured flames out from your parasol, shooting fire in a 30-foot line. Each creature in the line takes 10d6 fire damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). This activation loses its charge.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sparkshade Parasol

    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thawing Candle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2611)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 125

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This stubby, black candle has a wick made of a type of flammable bronze that can be found only on the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6). You activate the candle by lighting it, which enables creatures within 10 feet of the candle to ignore the cold while within range. Creatures in the area gain cold resistance 10. In addition, a creature taking [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) that is in or enters the area can immediately attempt a DC 15 flat check to end the persistent damage. A given creature can gain this flat check only once from a single _thawing candle_. Once lit, the candle burns for 10 minutes. If extinguished, it can't be relit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thawing Candle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Curious Teardrop](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2612)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 146

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +26; [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision 60 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112))

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +24, Alchemy [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26, Metal Lore +26, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +24

**Int** +6, **Wis** +2, **Cha** +4

**Will** +26 

</column>

---        

A _curious teardrop_, despite being a sphere of liquid metal, hangs like an earring on a golden finding. The intelligent droplet is a spirited chatterbox, always observing and taking mental notes; however, it's easily overwhelmed by new sights, which often reduce it to a sobbing mess. Occasionally, the _curious teardrop_ enters a stage of melancholy over the fragility of all matter, requiring immense reassurance to pull it out of its nihilism.

 The tear prefers not to talk about its past, though it claims to be an actual tear separated from its “parent.” Clerics of [Laudinmio](/Deities.aspx?ID=274) maintain that the elemental lord is the only being capable of shedding a perfect, sapient tear of metal.

**Activate—Request a Spell** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You ask for the _curious teardrop's_ assistance. It casts [_curse of lost time_](/Spells.aspx?ID=683), [_ferrous form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1369), or 7th-rank [_elemental form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=98) (metal elemental only), depending on your request.

**Activate—Reflect Emotions** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))  **Trigger** You're targeted by an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) or [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) effect;  **Effect** You receive a +4 status bonus to your saving throw against the triggering effect. Whether or not your save is successful, the teardrop attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check at +36 to immediately reflect a copy of the effect back at the originator, targeting it using the creature's own relevant statistics but controlling the effect as if the teardrop had cast it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Curious Teardrop

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Malleable](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2614)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 146

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a metal medium or heavy armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The metal of your armor can shift and rearrange at a moment's notice, allowing you to manipulate what kind of damage it resists.

**Activate—Reconfigure Armor** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** The armor's composition shifts, changing its [specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=211) group to a different one of your choice. This doesn't change what the armor is made of, and any runes or precious material it's made of apply to the new composition. Any property runes that can't apply to the new form are suppressed until the item takes a composition to which they can apply.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Malleable

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 146 **Price** 360 gp **Usage** held in 1 or 2 hands **Bulk** varies --- In its base form, this armament looks like a smooth bar of soft, gleaming metal shaped like a horseshoe. Its ability to adjust to any battle situation makes it a popular weapon for elite warriors of the [Plane of Metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505). It can be shaped into a melee weapon using its [_shifting_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=302) rune, but it can shift only into weapons primarily made of metal or back to its base shape. In weapon form, it's a +_1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) shifting weapon_. **Activate—Reshape** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** Strike with the _morphing weapon_, choosing one of the following benefits to apply to the Strike.
  • **Reach** Increase the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) by 5 feet.
  • **Shift** The weapon's damage becomes your choice of bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing instead of its normal type.
  • **Take** If the Strike hits, you can attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) or [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) from your target immediately after as a reaction.
  • **Weigh** Gain a status bonus to the damage roll equal to the weapon's number of damage dice.
name: Morphing Weapon
