markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Resonant Guitar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2616)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 146

**Price** 1,850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Every part of this dark, shining guitar, from the strings to the soundboard, is constructed of metal. When the strings are strummed, the gilding on the guitar ripples like a liquid. A _resonant guitar_ is a [virtuoso handheld musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37) that grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks attempted while using it.

 A _resonant guitar_ is a planar key for [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) and similar magic to travel to the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24). When using it in this way, you can play a tune inspired by your destination to make it more likely to arrive where you intend to be, appearing 1d6×25 miles from your intended destination instead of 1d10×25 miles away.

**Activate—Strum of Thunder** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You play a magnetic tune, enchanting one metallic weapon within 60 feet of you. This item gains the [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) rune for 1 hour.

**Activate—Chord of Protection** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet of you targets you or an ally with a melee attack;  **Effect** You strike a piercing chord, putting up an invisible sound barrier between the target and the attacker. The target gains a +2 status bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the Strike still hits, the barrier breaks, dealing 3d10 sonic damage to the attacker.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Resonant Guitar

[Rustbringer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2617)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=19)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The handle, chain, and spiked ball of this _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292) [flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=19)_ are all made of iron so rusted that the weapon appears nonfunctional at first glance. On Strikes against a creature that's primarily made of metal, it gains the [deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) trait.

**Activate—Rusting Disarm** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;   **Trigger** You critically succeed at a [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) attempt with the flail against a metal weapon;  **Effect** Flakes of acid-laden rust are deposited on the disarmed weapon. The weapon takes 2d6 damage, ignoring the weapon's Hardness.

The flail is a _+3 greater striking greater corrosive flail_, and the disarmed weapon takes 4d6 acid damage.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silver Snake Cane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2618)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 147

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This shimmery metal cane is both an assistive device and a popular companion for many zuhras. The cane's possessor can spend 1 minute feeding the cane an [elixir](/Traits.aspx?ID=59) or a dose of [ingested](/Traits.aspx?ID=95) or [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=97) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) to fill its venom sac. The cane can store only one such alchemical item at a time, and it expels the alchemical item when either 24 hours pass or it's fed a new alchemical item.

**Activate—Silver Snake Sword** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127))    **Effect** The silver snake cane becomes a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) [sword cane](/Weapons.aspx?ID=127)_ for 10 minutes. If the cane holds a dose of poison with the injury trait, that poison is automatically applied to the weapon. If the poison hasn't been expended by the time the cane turns back to normal, it remains stored in the cane. You can Dismiss this activation.

**Activate—Silver Snake Serum** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The _silver snake cane_ holds a dose of an alchemical item; **Effect** The silver snake cane bites a willing target of your choice within your reach, dealing 1 piercing damage and injecting the alchemical item. The item is expended, and if it was an elixir or ingested poison, the target is affected as though it consumed the item.

**Activate—True Silver Snake** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))    **Effect** The cane transforms into a [giant viper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=381) made of silver. All its Strikes are silver. It acts independently but obeys you. You can Dismiss this activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silver Snake Cane

>
[Spellsap Grenade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2619)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 147

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The mixture of reagents, liquid [djezet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1410), and solid metal shrapnel inside this grenade explodes on contact with air. A spellsap grenade deals the listed slashing damage and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage. On a hit against a prepared or spontaneous spellcaster, the target must succeed at a Will saving throw with the listed DC or lose one prepared spell or one spontaneous spell slot. The spell is randomly selected from among the caster's highest three spell ranks (and then from among the spells prepared in that rank, for a prepared spellcaster). The grenade grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 147 **Usage** affixed to a metal armor or weapon --- This perfectly octahedral magnetite crystal is covered in a hair-like layer of iron sand that always finds its way back to shape if wiped away. The spell attack modifier of any spell cast by Activating this item is +8, and the spell DC is 18.
  • **Armor** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and resistance 2 to [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) damage.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a spell by Activating the _spiny lodestone_, your Strikes with the weapon deal an additional 1d4 piercing damage until the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell **Effect** You cast [_needle darts_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1375). Spiny Lodestone **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 147 **Price** 90 gp Spiny Lodestone (Greater) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 147 **Price** 425 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 5, additional damage when affixed to a weapon is 1d6, the spell attack modifier is +14, and the spell DC is 24. **Activate** Cast a Spell **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_magnetic acceleration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=943). Spiny Lodestone (Major) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 147 **Price** 1,750 gp --- Bonus to Athletics checks when affixed to armor is +2, resistance when affixed to armor is 10, additional damage when affixed to a weapon is 1d8, the spell attack modifier is +19, and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-rank _magnetic acceleration_. **Activate** Cast a Spell **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_rust cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1377).
name: Spiny Lodestone

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 148 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This cylindrical iron staff has colored segments on both ends, one red and one blue. When you Strike with the staff, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the attack roll if the target is wearing metal armor or is primarily made of metal. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. Staff of Metal **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 148 **Price** 250 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrips** [_detect metal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1368)
  • **1st** [_conductive weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1367), [_shielded arm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1378)
  • **2nd** [_magnetic attraction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=944), [_magnetic repulsion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=945)
Staff of Metal (Greater) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 148 **Price** 900 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **3rd** [_magnetic acceleration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=943), [_noxious metals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1376)
  • **4th** [_mercurial stride_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1374), [_rust cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1377)
Staff of Metal (Major) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 148 **Price** 4,000 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **5th** magnetic repulsion, [_impaling spike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=697)
  • **6th** [_field of razors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1370), _magnetic acceleration_
name: Staff of Metal

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 148 **Price** 3,000 gp **Usage** worn gloves --- This elaborate metallic webbing feels soft when wrapped around your hands and forearms. It constantly shifts its strands and connections. The name of a zuhra shuyookh is etched in Talican on the only part of the item that's unchanging. You gain a +3 item bonus to your Reflex DC against attempts to Disarm an item you're holding in your hands. **Activate—Zuhra's Stratagem** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** You're wielding a weapon made primarily of metal; **Effect** You extend the weapon and call out the zuhra's name. They channel their magic through the gloves to assist you with their choice of offense or defense (as determined by the GM). The zuhra makes any choices for the spell, and any save DC is 30.
  • **Offense** The metal of the gloves wraps around your weapon and channels the zuhra's magic to cast a 6th-rank [_weapon storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=373) spell, replicating the metal weapon.
  • **Defense** The metal flows off your arms, creating a [_wall of metal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1379). The wall's surface has the same pattern as the gloves. You lose the gloves' item bonus until the barrier ends, at which point the metal returns to your hands and forearms. You can Dismiss the activation.
name: Zuhra's Gloves

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aboutface Figurehead](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2623)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Figurehead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=512" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 176

**Price** 980 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a ship's bow

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This otherwise plain-looking figurehead has the concerned expression of someone afraid they're being chased. If the vessel the figurehead is attached to has the sluggish vehicle ability, the figurehead suppresses that ability. Captains frequently give the ability to activate an _aboutface figurehead_ to the person at the helm of their ship.

**Activate—About Face!** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The figurehead turns its head as if to look behind it, spawning a momentary whirlpool under the ship and turbulent winds directly opposite the ship's heading. The ship makes a 180-degree turn in place, then continues heading in this new direction starting next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aboutface Figurehead

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Anglerfish Lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2624)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 176

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bull's-eye lantern is either stylized after an anglerfish or made from the taxidermy of one. While it can be lit as usual, the anglerfish lantern automatically shines when submerged in water.

**Activate—Mesmerizing Lights** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** All creatures within the bright light of the anglerfish lantern must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the light for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure). The fascination ends if the light is extinguished. [Aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=168) [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) and creatures with the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait take a –2 circumstance penalty to this check. Regardless of the result, the creature then becomes immune to this effect for the next 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Anglerfish Lantern
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 176

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Restricted"
>
Anglerfish Lantern (Submersible)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 176

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The DC is 24, and the lantern also has the following activation.

**Activate—Dive!** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You lower the _submersible anglerfish lantern_ into water at least 15 feet deep while issuing a command. The lantern transforms into a [bathysphere](/Vehicles.aspx?ID=38) for 1 hour. This vehicle possesses a 60-foot cone light that can be swiveled up to 90 degrees with an Interact action and has the activation listed above. When the effect ends, any occupants are ejected harmlessly. If the bathysphere becomes broken, the effect ends and the _submersible anglerfish lantern_ is broken as well.
name: Anglerfish Lantern

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Brine Dragon Scale](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2625)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 176

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a melee weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Reaction" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** A creature in reach of the weapon takes an action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. 

 
</row>

</column>

---

[Brine dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=175) are known to distract their foes at just the right moment, and this blue-green scale appears to have come from one of these creatures. When you activate the scale, it cracks open and releases a spray of caustic saltwater at the triggering creature. The creature takes 2d8 acid damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. If the creature critically fails at its save, its concentration is broken—the triggering action is disrupted.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Brine Dragon Scale

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Conch of Otherworldly Seas](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2626)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 176

**Price** 1,850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Magical writing covers the surface of this beautiful conch shell, which emits a blue light from inside. A _conch of otherworldly seas_ is a [virtuoso handheld musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37) that grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks attempted while using it. 

The conch is a planar key for [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) and similar magic to travel to the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7). When using it this way, you can attune it to the waters of your destination to make it more likely to arrive where you intend to be, appearing 1d6×25 miles from your intended destination instead of 1d10×25 miles away.

**Activate—Voice of Oceans** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You hold the horn to your ear and can understand and speak [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) as long as it remains there.

**Activate—Sounds of the Deep** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You hold the horn to your ear and touch the correct series of runes inscribed on its surface, causing the conch to cast a 5th-rank [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=39) spell for your benefit. Provided you choose a location that's underwater, you can extend the spell's range to 1 mile and hear with perfect clarity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Conch of Otherworldly Seas

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Faydhaan's Dallah](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2627)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 176

**Price** 4,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Noble faydhaan shuyookhs serve coffee brewed in ornate dallahs such as this one to welcome guests in their courts. Most nobles have their kitchen staff import the beans at great expense from other planes then roast the beans with local ingredients gathered on the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7) to create a brew unique to their locale. A faydhaan's dallah has the name of a specific faydhaan shuyookh engraved on the bottom and typically comes with six drinking cups, a platter, and a selection of coffee beans.

**Activate—Brew** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You place a handful of beans in the dallah, and it immediately grinds the beans, fills with water, and brews six cups' worth of coffee. Pouring a cupful of coffee into a vessel takes an Interact action, as does drinking a cup. A creature that drinks a cup can breathe underwater and is protected from [mild or severe temperatures](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) of underwater environments for 8 hours. The coffee can later be transmuted using the dallah's other activation, but this latent magic ends if the dallah is used to brew coffee again.

 Coffee poured from the dallah stays in its vessel until someone drinks it or deliberately pours it out, even if the vessel is in an environment where the coffee would normally escape, such as underwater.

**Activate—Faydhaan's Hospitality** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You used the dallah's activation to brew a batch of coffee today; **Effect** You speak the name of the faydhaan shuyookh inscribed on the dallah. You don't need to be holding the dallah to use this activation. The shuyookh transmutes the coffee within the body of each creature who partook from the batch you brewed, choosing a single <a href="https://2e.aonprd.com/Search.aspx?include-item-subcategories=potions&amp;include-rarities=common&amp;values-to=level%3A6&amp;display=list" class="internal-link">common potion of 6th level or lower &lt;%END>, which grants them all the benefits of that potion. Typically, the shuyookh chooses a [moderate healing potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=186), [lesser potion of resistance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=192), or [potion of swimming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=193).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Faydhaan's Dallah

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kraken Figurehead](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2628)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Figurehead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=512" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 177

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a ship's bow

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A knot of tentacles makes up the body of this figurehead. It's always slightly slimy and sticky to the touch. Superstitious sailors tend to avoid ships with this figurehead on the bow, claiming it's bad luck to flaunt a [kraken's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=275) image while at sea.

**Activate—Lash Out!** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The effigy causes spectral tentacles to erupt from the ship's hull. These tentacles lash out in an emanation with a distance equal to the ship's length and remain active for 10 minutes. Creatures in the aura and in the same body of water as the ship take a –2 circumstance penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) as the writhing tentacles lash out in all directions.

**Activate—Grab Them!** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))   **Requirements** The ship's spectral tentacles are activated; **Effect** The tentacles grab at an enemy vessel in the aura. Attempt a piloting check against the target vessel's AC. On a success, the tentacles grab hold and tether the two vessels together. While tethered, the ships can't move farther away from each other, creatures aboard the enemy vessel receive a –2 circumstance penalty to all piloting checks, and creatures attempting to Board the enemy vessel gain a +2 circumstance bonus to any check required to do so. 

If you use this activation while the tentacles are already latched onto another ship, the tentacles instead pull that ship toward yours a distance up to half your ship's swim Speed with a success (or up to your ship's full swim Speed with a critical success).

 The pilot of the enemy ship can attempt a DC 27 piloting check to break free as a single action that has the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15) trait. With a success, the ship frees itself from the tentacles.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Kraken Figurehead
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 177

**Price** 490 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Kraken Figurehead (Wracking)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 177

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The DC to break free of the tentacles is 31. When you use the figurehead's second activation to pull an enemy ship, even if you didn't pull the ship any actual distance, you can have the tentacles try to crack open its hull. The tentacles deal 7d10 bludgeoning damage to the enemy vessel (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). Apply the ship's Hardness as normal.
name: Kraken Figurehead

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

**Base Weapon** [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Colorful stripes and trailing ribbons give this _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [underwater](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2634) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=309) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_ an appearance like a poisonous lionfish. While holding the spear, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletic](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to swim.

**Activate—Lionfish Poison** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You successfully Strike a creature with the _lionfish spear_;  **Effect** The spear injects lionfish poison into the target; **Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (1 round); **Stage 2** 5d6 poison damage and sickened 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 2d4 hours

While holding the spear, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletic](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to swim.

**Activate—Lionfish Poison** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You successfully Strike a creature with the _lionfish spear_;  **Effect** The spear injects lionfish poison into the target; **Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage and [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (1 round); **Stage 2** 5d6 poison damage and sickened 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 2d4 hours

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 178 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Eight flailing octopus arms covered in suckers pop out from the sides of your body when you imbibe this potion. The arms share your multiple attack penalty and attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a random enemy within 15 feet of you. On a success, roll 1d4 to determine an additional effect of the arms, which lasts as long as the target remains grabbed or restrained by the arms. On subsequent turns, you can use a single action, which has the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15) trait, to have the arms either Grapple a creature currently [grappled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) (with no added effect) or release any creature they currently hold and repeat their initial effect. After 1 minute, the arms disappear and the potion's effects end.
**1d4****Added Effect**
1The arms cover the eyes; the target is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1).
2The arms cover the mouth; the target can't speak.
3The arms wrap around the limbs; the flat check for the target to use a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) action while grabbed is DC 7 instead of DC 5.
4 The arms crush your enemy; the target takes bludgeoning damage equal to the potion's level immediately and at the end of each of its turns.
Octopus Potion (Lesser) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 178 **Price** 50 gp **Bulk** L --- The Athletics modifier is +12 and the [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC is 20. Octopus Potion (Moderate) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 178 **Price** 130 gp **Bulk** L --- The Athletics modifier is +17 and the Escape DC is 25. Octopus Potion (Greater) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 178 **Price** 500 gp **Bulk** L --- The Athletics modifier is +22 and the Escape DC is 30.
name: Octopus Potion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sharkskin Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2631)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 178

**Price** 1,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn clothing

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sandy-textured robe comes with sleeves that resemble dorsal fins. It's believed to have been developed by frustrated alchemists from the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) for trips to the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7). The sharkskin robe grants you a swim Speed equal to your land Speed and a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks.

**Activate—Shark's Elegance** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** For 1 minute, any time you make a Strike, your weapon or [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack gains the benefit of the [_underwater_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2634) weapon property rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sharkskin Robe

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shell of Easy Breathing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2632)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 178

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large salt-encrusted seashell is more than 2 feet across, with images of deep sea creatures carved around its edge.

**Activate—Fill the Shell** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You place the shell on a level surface and sprinkle a few drops of water into its basin. The shell slowly fills with saltwater over the course of 1 minute. The shell's magic then becomes active, indicated by a steady stream of bubbles. Moving the shell disturbs its contents, causing the item to deactivate and the water inside to evaporate; otherwise, it remains activated for an unlimited duration.

 While the shell is activated, a creature can submerge its head and let water and bubbles fill its nose and mouth (or whatever body part it uses for breathing) as a 3-action activity. For the next hour, the creature can breathe underwater. The creature is then temporarily immune to _shells of easy breathing_ until the next time it makes its daily preparations.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Shell of Easy Breathing
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 178

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Shell of Easy Breathing (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 178

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

The duration of the ability to breathe underwater increases to 8 hours.
name: Shell of Easy Breathing

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Veiled Figurehead](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2635)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Figurehead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=512" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 179

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a ship's bow

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This figurehead is carved in the shape of a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91), but it has no facial features whatsoever.

**Activate—Veil!** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You change the appearance of the ship in minor but noticeable ways. Its general size and shape can't be changed, but you can alter surface details to your liking. Flags and sails can be recolored and given new markings, and the overall material of the ship can appear a different color or quality. Wear and surface damage (like small holes, tears, and burns) can be masked to make the vessel look unblemished, or you can create such damage and wear. The figurehead itself shifts to fit the change and gains a face to match the rest of the ship. The illusion lasts for 6 hours or until you Dismiss this effect.

 Any creature that boards the ship or uses the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to examine it disbelieves the illusion if it succeeds at a DC 25 Perception check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Veiled Figurehead
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 179

**Price** 335 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Veiled Figurehead (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 179

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The DC to disbelieve is 33, and the illusion lasts up to 24 hours. Activating a _greater veiled figurehead_ also extends the illusion to those on the ship. The figurehead casts a [_veil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=355) spell upon them, except it targets everyone on board when activated, and it alters their clothing to a general look that matches the new appearance of the ship. In addition, it can make everyone appear as a specific ancestry, but you must choose the same one for all targets. This effect ends for any target who leaves the ship and ends for all targets if the illusion on the ship ends. A creature that disbelieves the illusion for the ship or any disguised crew member disbelieves the entire illusion.
name: Veiled Figurehead

Its general size and shape can't be changed, but you can alter surface details to your liking. Flags and sails can be recolored and given new markings, and the overall material of the ship can appear a different color or quality. Wear and surface damage (like small holes, tears, and burns) can be masked to make the vessel look unblemished, or you can create such damage and wear. The figurehead itself shifts to fit the change and gains a face to match the rest of the ship. The illusion lasts for 6 hours or until you Dismiss this effect.

 Any creature that boards the ship or uses the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to examine it disbelieves the illusion if it succeeds at a DC 25 Perception check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Veiled Figurehead
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 179

**Price** 335 gp

<row gap="medium">

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Veiled Figurehead (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 179

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The DC to disbelieve is 33, and the illusion lasts up to 24 hours. Activating a _greater veiled figurehead_ also extends the illusion to those on the ship. The figurehead casts a [_veil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=355) spell upon them, except it targets everyone on board when activated, and it alters their clothing to a general look that matches the new appearance of the ship. In addition, it can make everyone appear as a specific ancestry, but you must choose the same one for all targets. This effect ends for any target who leaves the ship and ends for all targets if the illusion on the ship ends. A creature that disbelieves the illusion for the ship or any disguised crew member disbelieves the entire illusion.
name: Veiled Figurehead

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Animal Nip](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2636)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Olfactory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=246" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Animal nip contains a mix of herbaceous, fragrant plants, ground into a coarse powder with a strong scent that attracts a broad spectrum of animals. You activate animal nip by sprinkling it on the ground or a target of your choice. For the next minute, all creatures within 30 feet that have the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) trait must attempt a Will save or become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the smell of the animal nip for 1 round. On a critical failure, they also fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and roll about on the ground. If the target is subject to a [hostile act](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), the fascination ends immediately. Regardless of the result of the creature's save, it's then immune to animal nip for 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="medium">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blooming Lotus Seed Pod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2637)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200

**Price** 68 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The seeds of this lotus seed pod scatter with incredible ease and accuracy, quickly growing into temporary plants.

**Activate—Floating Pads** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You scatter the _blooming lotus seed pod's_ seeds across a non-damaging liquid surface within 40 feet of you, where they form into 5 lotus pads that each float in place in a separate square within range for 1 minute. Each lotus pad has a 10- foot radius and can support 1 Large creature, 2 Medium creatures, or 4 Small creatures.

**Activate—Blooming Flower** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You plant the _blooming lotus seed pod_ in the ground and a giant lotus flower blooms in that square. Over the next 8 hours, creatures who sleep for at least 6 hours within 30 feet of the lotus flower gain the benefits of long-term rest as though they'd spent an entire day and night resting, and all creatures within the affected area are immune to the effects of the [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) spell and other magical effects that affect only sleeping creatures.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blooming Lotus Seed Pod

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) When using a broadleaf shield, the season is the current season where the player is located._ **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- Though it resembles an enormous leaf freshly plucked from a tree, a _broadleaf shield_ is extremely durable. It also regrows rapidly, fully repairing itself when broken if it's left in sunlight for 10 consecutive minutes. At each dawn, the leaf transforms to an appearance appropriate for the season. While you're wielding the shield, you gain resistance to a damage type depending on the shield's color. This resistance doubles while you have the shield raised.
  • **Pink** (void resistance) In spring, the leaf is a delicate blossom-like pink and exudes the energy of life.
  • **Green** (fire resistance) In summer, the leaf is a deep, rich green with a waxy coating.
  • **Orange** (poison resistance) In autumn, the leaf turns orange, red, or yellow and dehydrates slightly.
  • **Brown** (cold resistance) In winter, the leaf turns dead, dry, and brown.
**Activate—Change Season** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The shield transforms as though living through seasons in a moment, becoming the color of your choice for 5 minutes. Broadleaf Shield **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 **Price** 225 gp **Bulk** L --- The shield has Hardness 4, HP 16, and BT 8. The resistances are 3 (6 when raised). Broadleaf Shield (Greater) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 **Price** 675 gp **Bulk** L --- The shield has Hardness 6, HP 24, and BT 12. The resistances are 4 (8 when raised). Broadleaf Shield (Major) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 **Price** 2,750 gp **Bulk** L --- The shield has Hardness 8, HP 32, and BT 16. The resistances are 5 (10 when raised). markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) When using a broadleaf shield, the season is the current season where the player is located._ **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- Though it resembles an enormous leaf freshly plucked from a tree, a _broadleaf shield_ is extremely durable. It also regrows rapidly, fully repairing itself when broken if it's left in sunlight for 10 consecutive minutes. At each dawn, the leaf transforms to an appearance appropriate for the season. While you're wielding the shield, you gain resistance to a damage type depending on the shield's color. This resistance doubles while you have the shield raised.
  • **Pink** (void resistance) In spring, the leaf is a delicate blossom-like pink and exudes the energy of life.
  • **Green** (fire resistance) In summer, the leaf is a deep, rich green with a waxy coating.
  • **Orange** (poison resistance) In autumn, the leaf turns orange, red, or yellow and dehydrates slightly.
  • **Brown** (cold resistance) In winter, the leaf turns dead, dry, and brown.
**Activate—Change Season** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The shield transforms as though living through seasons in a moment, becoming the color of your choice for 5 minutes. Broadleaf Shield **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 **Price** 225 gp **Bulk** L --- The shield has Hardness 4, HP 16, and BT 8. The resistances are 3 (6 when raised). Broadleaf Shield (Greater) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 **Price** 675 gp **Bulk** L --- The shield has Hardness 6, HP 24, and BT 12. The resistances are 4 (8 when raised). Broadleaf Shield (Major) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 **Price** 2,750 gp **Bulk** L --- The shield has Hardness 8, HP 32, and BT 16. The resistances are 5 (10 when raised). Broadleaf Shield (True) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 **Price** 13,750 gp **Bulk** L --- The shield has Hardness 10, HP 40, and BT 20. The resistances are 6 (12 when raised).
name: Broadleaf Shield

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 200 **Price** 10 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand --- Named because of its popularity among thieves to distract any authorities in pursuit, a captivating rosebud has a near-irresistible fragrance. **Activate—Scent of Roses** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) **Effect** You throw the _captivating rosebud_ in a square adjacent to you. The rosebud quickly sprouts into a little rosebush that lasts for 1 hour. Any creature that passes within 15 feet of the rose bush, other than yourself, must attempt a DC 18 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks for 10 minutes.
**Failure** As success, but a –2 penalty.
**Critical Failure** As success, but a –2 penalty and the creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the rosebush. **Activate—Rose Vines** 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Effect** You plant the _captivating rosebud_ into a square adjacent to a building or other structure. It grows into a rosebush that stretches up to 30 feet tall. You and your allies can use the rosebush as a ladder to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) easily up and down the side of the adjacent structure, but all other creatures must succeed at a DC 17 Will saving throw or fail to notice the rosebush's presence.
name: Captivating Rosebud

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Carver-Cutter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2640)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 201

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Battle Axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Many of the experienced woodcarvers of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) learn to fight as well, the better to travel into hazardous areas in search of rare and beautiful types of wood for their craft. A _carver-cutter_ serves a dual role as a weapon and a tool for felling trees and engraving wood. The _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16)_ looks like an exquisitely made woodcutting axe.

**Activate—Carving Chisel** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You pull free a woodworking chisel stored in the knob of the axe. It can have any standard chisel shape you imagine as you activate this ability. You get a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks for woodworking if you use chisels from the _carver-cutter_. The chisel also functions as a [main-gauche](/Weapons.aspx?ID=32). All runes on the axe are replicated on the chisel, but only while both items are on the same person. You can Interact to reinsert the chisel.

**Activate—Chop Down** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Make a Strike with the axe against a [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature or a creature made primarily of wood. This Strike deals an additional 2d6 precision damage, and on a hit, the creature is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Carver-Cutter
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 201

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Carver-Cutter (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 201

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The axe is a _+2 greater striking battle axe_, and the precision damage is 3d6.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Carver-Cutter (Major)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 201

**Price** 5,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The axe is a _+2 [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=300) greater striking battle axe_, and the precision damage is 3d6.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Carver-Cutter (True)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 201

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The axe is a _+3 keen greater striking battle axe_, the Crafting bonus is +3, and the precision damage is 4d6.
name: Carver-Cutter

**Activate—Chop Down** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Make a Strike with the axe against a [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature or a creature made primarily of wood. This Strike deals an additional 2d6 precision damage, and on a hit, the creature is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Glowing Lantern Fruit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2641)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 201

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The flesh of this fruit pod resembles a stylized paper lantern, with a woody, geometric structure and thin layers of flesh, complete with a glow coming from the interior.

**Activate—Lantern Light** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** _The glowing lantern_ fruit sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 60-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 60 feet) for 8 hours. While the light is shining, you can Interact with the _glowing lantern fruit_ to open or close some of its reflective leaves, making the light directional like a bull's-eye [lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=29) or a hooded lantern.

**Activate—Fire Fruit** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You plant the _glowing lantern fruit_ in the ground upside down. The petals of the lantern peel away, while the fruit inside glows hotter. For the next 8 hours, the _glowing lantern fruit_ emits as much light and heat as a bonfire, giving all creatures within 15 feet immunity to the effects of [mild and severe cold temperatures](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) for as long as they're within the area.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Glowing Lantern Fruit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kizidhar's Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2642)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 201

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [standard-grade duskwood shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=312) is perfectly symmetrical and incredibly sturdy despite its elaborate construction. These shields are typically given to the chosen emissaries of a [kizidhar shuyookh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2682), and the shuyookh's name and title are engraved in [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) behind the shield's handle.

**Activate—Repair** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You command the shield to repair itself, awakening the restorative magic within. For the next minute, the shield heals 3 Hit Points each round at the start of your turn.

**Activate—Secure Site** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You place the _kizidhar's shield_ into the ground at the edge of your campsite, which can't be more than 30 feet on a side, then speak the name of the kizidhar shuyookh carved into its handle. The shuyookh briefly appears just long enough to make a sweeping gesture, causing sprawling thorns to grow in an unnaturally symmetrical formation around the campsite, protecting it for the next 8 hours with an effect that's otherwise identical to [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=366). All creatures within the campsite are then immediately affected by a three-action [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell cast at 4th rank by the shield. After 8 hours, the wall rots away, and the shield can be retrieved as an Interact action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kizidhar's Shield

---

A phrase of luck is carved into the handle of this wooden teaspoon. While a variety of other cutlery with similar properties exists, a spoon is often the most convenient and inconspicuous to carry.

**Activate—Purify** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You stir the spoon in food or drink, casting [_cleanse cuisine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=241) on the substance as you stir. This small spoon can purify up to 1 gallon of food or drink.

This small spoon can purify up to 1 gallon of food or drink.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sandalwood Fan](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2645)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ornate fan's carving depicts a particular tableau from the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25), often of the site where the fan was created. Fanning it through the air creates a sandalwood-scented breeze and a crackling of magic. A _sandalwood fan_ is a planar key for [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) and similar magic. The _sandalwood fan_ makes it more likely for you to arrive where you intended, appearing 1d6×25 miles from your intended destination instead of the usual error range. This distance is further reduced to 1d4×25 miles if your destination is the scenery depicted on the fan.

**Activate—Plant Speech** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _sandalwood fan_ casts [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) and translate for [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) and [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) on you. In addition to conversing with nearby plants, you can also communicate with any living plants you've spoken with in the past using this fan. These plants must either be on the same plane as you or on the Plane of Wood.

**Activate—Cloud of Leaves** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You summon leaves that protect your allies and identify enemies. All allies and indifferent creatures within 30 feet of you gain [lesser cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) for 1 round, while enemies come under the effect of [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=107) for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sandalwood Fan

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Splintering Spear](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2646)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The entirety of this _+2 [duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=376) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_ is made of worn, cracked, splintered wood, including the spearhead. These splinters never harm you when you hold the weapon, but when you hit with the spear, splinters break off in the target, dealing 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

**Activate—Shatter** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You smash the spear into the ground or another nearby surface, shattering it into thousands of duskwood splinters in your choice of a 30-foot cone or a 10-foot emanation. Each creature in the area takes 12d6 piercing damage with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that fails also takes the same amount of persistent bleed damage as the spear deals on a hit. The spear regrows into its full form at the end of this turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Splintering Spear](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2646)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The entirety of this _+2 [duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=376) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_ is made of worn, cracked, splintered wood, including the spearhead. These splinters never harm you when you hold the weapon, but when you hit with the spear, splinters break off in the target, dealing 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

**Activate—Shatter** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You smash the spear into the ground or another nearby surface, shattering it into thousands of duskwood splinters in your choice of a 30-foot cone or a 10-foot emanation. Each creature in the area takes 12d6 piercing damage with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that fails also takes the same amount of persistent bleed damage as the spear deals on a hit. The spear regrows into its full form at the end of this turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tailor's Boll](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2647)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Harvested from one of the rainbow fields of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25), this cotton boll is filled with multicolored fibers. When you Activate the boll, you make a request for a bespoke set of non-magical [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2) or [fine clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=15). The hard fibers and seeds leap from your hand to spin and weave, cotton flying through the air at incredible speed to make cloth and thread. The clothing is ready at the start of your next turn, in the most convenient spot nearby for hanging the clothing or laying it flat.

 If you hold clothing in your other hand when you activate the boll, the boll instead reweaves that item to match the style you specified. This can't change the type of item; you couldn't turn fine clothing into explorer's clothing or winter clothing, for example.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tailor's Boll

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tales in Timber](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2648)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Wooden Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=36)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Dozens of pictorial stories—told in wood carvings, painting, or pyrography—adorn every outer surface of this _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [wooden breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=36)_. All are old, and many truly ancient, depicting tales set eons ago on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). You gain a +2 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26). If you know the [_collective memories_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=15) ritual, you can use Nature instead of [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) if you're the primary caster.

**Activate—Statue Disguise** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor makes you look like a wooden statue version of one of the creatures depicted on the armor for 1 hour. This is a 3rd-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), but it can make you look like any creature depicted on the armor. This doesn't change your size or the capabilities of your body, and it scales the appearance of the creature to match yours. You can Dismiss the activation. This disguise is just real enough to make you somewhat closer to a creature of wood. You're affected by two spells while it lasts: [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20) and [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294). If you stand still, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to appear as an inanimate wooden statue, in addition to the status bonus to Deception from _illusory disguise_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tales in Timber
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

>
Tales in Timber (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tales in Timber (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

The armor is _+2 greater resilient wooden breastplate_, the item bonus to Nature checks is +3, and the _oaken resilience_ is 6th rank.
name: Tales in Timber

<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 202

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Wooden Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=36)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Dozens of pictorial stories—told in wood carvings, painting, or pyrography—adorn every outer surface of this _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [wooden breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=36)_. All are old, and many truly ancient, depicting tales set eons ago on the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). You gain a +2 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26). If you know the [_collective memories_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=15) ritual, you can use Nature instead of [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) if you're the primary caster.

**Activate—Statue Disguise** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor makes you look like a wooden statue version of one of the creatures depicted on the armor for 1 hour. This is a 3rd-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), but it can make you look like any creature depicted on the armor. This doesn't change your size or the capabilities of your body, and it scales the appearance of the creature to match yours. You can Dismiss the activation. This disguise is just real enough to make you somewhat closer to a creature of wood. You're affected by two spells while it lasts: [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20) and [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294). If you stand still, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to appear as an inanimate wooden statue, in addition to the status bonus to Deception from _illusory disguise_.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Therapeutic Snap Peas](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2649)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 203

**Price** 85 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Affectionately called “the healer's kit of the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25),” _therapeutic snap peas_ are specially cultivated snap pea pods overflowing with restorative magic.

**Activate—Healing Pod** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You break the snap pea pod in half, unleashing its restorative healing energy. Up to 5 creatures of your choice within 30 feet regain 4d8+10 Hit Points and gain a +2 item bonus to all saves against [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) or [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) for 1 minute.

**Activate—Beanstalk** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You plant the _therapeutic snap peas_ in a square of open ground, after which they rapidly grow into a 10-foot-tall beanstalk that remains in place for 8 hours. The beanstalk's enormous pea pods provide a full day's food for up to 8 living creatures of size Large or smaller, which must be eaten before the beanstalk expires. Any creature that eats the pea pods also immediately heals 30 Hit Points and can attempt a new saving throw against one poison or disease afflicting them.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Therapeutic Snap Peas

  • **Weapon** After you cast a [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) spell by activating the triad, thorns erupt from the weapon. Your Strikes with the weapon deal an additional 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) until the end of your next turn.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Versatile Tinderbox](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2651)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 203

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A fine case carved from elegant wood, this tinderbox holds twigs and strips of wood in a selection of six colors. In a typical _versatile tinderbox_, these colors are black, blue, green, magenta, yellow, and violet. When used in lighting a fire, colored tinder alters the flames' color and smoke to match. The box is perfectly carved and constructed to hold tinder, keeping it completely dry, but is incapable of closing if used to hold anything else. The tinderbox replenishes itself; it's never out of tinder when its owner is in need, but it never produces a surplus of tinder either.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Versatile Tinderbox

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cipher of the Elemental Planes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2652)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 224

**Price** 9,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This device is made from two metal discs, one slightly smaller than the other, each bearing a variety of runes and symbols along their outer edges. The center ring typically shows a rune for each elemental plane, and many older ciphers include only the planes of air, earth, fire, and water. A thick, golden pin in the center of both discs holds them together. 

**Activate—Align to Plane** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=141), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163))    **Effect** You turn the discs to align symbols, creating a minute planar gateway as large as a keyhole. You can look through it to view a location in an elemental plane. Each cipher connects to 12 locations on each elemental plane—typically large settlements. Anyone holding the cipher can understand the primary language of the plane the cipher is aligned to. A _cipher of the planes_ can be used in place of a planar key for [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) and similar magic for travel to the plane it's aligned to. When it's used this way, you arrive unerringly at the location the cipher is aligned to.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cipher of the Elemental Planes

</row>

</column>

---

This chunk of solidified planar energy is as large as a walnut and comes in six different varieties: a clear piece of crystal for the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4), a rough piece of rock for the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5), a solidified piece of cooled lava for the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=6), a compact piece of iron for the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24), a solid piece of ice for the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=7), or a compact mass of plant matter for the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). You crack the fragment as you activate it, unleashing the planar energy within. This energy casts a spell of your choice: a 5th-rank [_elemental form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=98) spell affecting you or a 5th-rank [_summon elemental_](/Spells.aspx?ID=320) spell; if you summon an elemental, you can Sustain the activation to keep control of the elemental. The spell's element matches that of the fragment.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gate Attenuator](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2654)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 224

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Gate attenuators_ are typically worn near the body's core and are  shaped like portals or passageways, making literal the elemental gates [kineticists](/Classes.aspx?ID=23) possess within their bodies. The appearance can vary from a simple disk with a hole in the middle to a design matching a city gate of a particular settlement. If you're a kineticist, the attenuator grants you a +1 item bonus to your impulse attack modifier (but not to your impulse DC). When you invest a _gate attenuator_, attune it to one element of your choice. Designs on the attenuator's surface transform to match that element, and the attenuator gains the element's trait until it's no longer invested or is attuned to a different element.

**Activate—Elemental Spell** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _gate attenuator_ casts a 1st-rank spell, with a spell attack modifier of +7 and spell DC of 17. If you're a kineticist and the spell's element matches one of your kinetic elements, you can use your impulse attack modifier instead of the spell attack modifier or your impulse DC instead of the spell DC. The spell corresponds to the element the item is attuned to, and it gains that element's trait if it doesn't already have it: **air** [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143), **earth** [_pummeling rubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=708), **fire** [_dehydrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1351), **metal** [_thunderstrike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=283), **water** [_snowball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=540), or **wood** [_flourishing flora_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1401).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Gate Attenuator
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 224

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Gate Attenuator (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 224

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to your impulse attack modifier is +2. The activation casts a 5th-rank spell, with a spell attack modifier of +18 and a spell DC of 28: **air** [_pressure zone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1318), **earth** [_sand form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1340), **fire** [_flames of ego_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1357), **metal** [_impaling spike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=697), **water** [_freezing rain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1388), or **wood** [_entwined roots_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1400).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Gate Attenuator (Major)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=532" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 224

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A _major gate attenuator_ has the [apex](/Traits.aspx?ID=10) trait. When you invest it, you either increase your Constitution modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher score. The item bonus to your impulse attack modifier is +2. The activation casts an 8th-rank spell, with a spell attack modifier of +27 and a spell DC of 37: **air** [_whirlwind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1033), **earth** [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=95), **fire** [_boil blood_](/Spells.aspx?ID=874), **metal** [_rust cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1377) (8th rank), **water** [_whirlpool_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1398), or **wood** [_pollen pods_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1407) (8th rank).
name: Gate Attenuator

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 224

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Gate attenuators_ are typically worn near the body's core and are  shaped like portals or passageways, making literal the elemental gates [kineticists](/Classes.aspx?ID=23) possess within their bodies. The appearance can vary from a simple disk with a hole in the middle to a design matching a city gate of a particular settlement. If you're a kineticist, the attenuator grants you a +1 item bonus to your impulse attack modifier (but not to your impulse DC). When you invest a _gate attenuator_, attune it to one element of your choice. Designs on the attenuator's surface transform to match that element, and the attenuator gains the element's trait until it's no longer invested or is attuned to a different element.

**Activate—Elemental Spell** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _gate attenuator_ casts a 1st-rank spell, with a spell attack modifier of +7 and spell DC of 17. If you're a kineticist and the spell's element matches one of your kinetic elements, you can use your impulse attack modifier instead of the spell attack modifier or your impulse DC instead of the spell DC. The spell corresponds to the element the item is attuned to, and it gains that element's trait if it doesn't already have it: **air** [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143), **earth** [_pummeling rubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=708), **fire** [_dehydrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1351), **metal** [_thunderstrike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=283), **water** [_snowball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=540), or **wood** [_flourishing flora_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1401).

</column>

---

The item bonus to your impulse attack modifier is +2. The activation casts a 5th-rank spell, with a spell attack modifier of +18 and a spell DC of 28: **air** [_pressure zone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1318), **earth** [_sand form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1340), **fire** [_flames of ego_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1357), **metal** [_impaling spike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=697), **water** [_freezing rain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1388), or **wood** [_entwined roots_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1400).

---

A _major gate attenuator_ has the [apex](/Traits.aspx?ID=10) trait. When you invest it, you either increase your Constitution modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher score. The item bonus to your impulse attack modifier is +2. The activation casts an 8th-rank spell, with a spell attack modifier of +27 and a spell DC of 37: **air** [_whirlwind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1033), **earth** [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=95), **fire** [_boil blood_](/Spells.aspx?ID=874), **metal** [_rust cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1377) (8th rank), **water** [_whirlpool_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1398), or **wood** [_pollen pods_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1407) (8th rank).
