markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jann's Prism](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2655)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 224

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Light always seems to be refracting through this simple prism, creating a hazy multicolor aura that surrounds the glass. When exposed to direct sunlight, the prism radiates a beam of light that shifts in color. This beam spells out the name of the jann shuyookh for whom the prism was designed. While holding the prism to your eye, your vision becomes overwhelmed with colors that guide your eye, granting you a +2 item bonus to visual Perception checks. If you look through the prism while you Seek, you can scan or search an area twice as large as normal (a 60-foot cone, 30-foot burst, or 20-foot square) as the varying colors help you distinguish between your surroundings.

**Activate—Jann's Light** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You raise the prism above your head and call upon the jann shuyookh to come to your aid. The shuyookh's face becomes visible in a reflection in the prism and light shines out from the prism, surrounding you in a multitude of colors. For 1 minute, you shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot emanation (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet). The light coruscates with two colors chosen by the jann, and you gain resistance 5 to two damage types based on the colors chosen: **red** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), **orange** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), **yellow** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), **green** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), **blue** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147), **indigo** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), or **violet** [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Jann's Prism

May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This shield is grown entirely of specially treated fungi, giving it the appearance of a frilly disc.
When left atop at least 1 bulk of organic matter, it gains fast healing 1; after 10 minutes 1 Bulk of the organic matter has been consumed by the shield. This shield doubles its hardness against bludgeoning, electricity, and sonic damage. **Activate** manipulate; **Trigger** The shield is broken; **Effect** The shield spews spores into one adjacent square, dealing persistent poison damage to all creatures in within it; a successful Fortitude save negates the damage. **Craft Requirements** You are trained in Nature, and can cast a fungus spell or are fungus. Mycoweave Shield (Lesser) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 **Price** 40 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The shield has Hardness 2, HP 12, and BT 6. When the shield breaks, the reaction deals 1d6 persistent poison damage with a DC 16 Fortitude saving throw. Mycoweave Shield (Greater) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 **Price** 40 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The shield has Hardness 4, HP 64, and BT 32. When the shield breaks, the reaction deals 2d6 persistent poison damage with a DC 24 Fortitude saving throw. Mycoweave Shield (Major) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 **Price** 40 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The shield has Hardness 6, HP 110, and BT 55. When the shield breaks, the reaction deals 3d6 persistent poison damage with a DC 35 Fortitude saving throw.
name: Mycoweave Shield

When left atop at least 1 bulk of organic matter, it gains fast healing 1; after 10 minutes 1 Bulk of the organic matter has been consumed by the shield. This shield doubles its hardness against bludgeoning, electricity, and sonic damage. **Activate** manipulate; **Trigger** The shield is broken; **Effect** The shield spews spores into one adjacent square, dealing persistent poison damage to all creatures in within it; a successful Fortitude save negates the damage. **Craft Requirements** You are trained in Nature, and can cast a fungus spell or are fungus. Mycoweave Shield (Lesser) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 **Price** 40 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The shield has Hardness 2, HP 12, and BT 6. May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The DC of this item is 18_ **Price** 8 gp --- This snare is designed to divert a target's movement towards another snare or hazard. You may choose to have the target attempt a Will saving throw instead of a Reflex saving throw; if you do, add the fear and mental traits plus add either the visual or auditory trait.
When a Small or larger creature enters the square, the snare Pushes the creature 5 feet and the target becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) 1 until the end of their next turn. On a critical failure the snare Pushes the target 10 feet and the target becomes clumsy 2 until the end of their next turn.
name: Setup Snare

May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This staff is constructed from a magically grown amanita mushroom, with a shaft that spreads into a bright red cap speckled in white. While wielding the staff you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Nature checks to identify fungus. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Spore Shepherd's Staff **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 **Price** 100 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_puff of poison_](/Spells.aspx?ID=977)
  • **1st** [_protector tree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=976) (resembles giant mushroom, not a tree), [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325) (fungus only)
Spore Shepherd's Staff (Greater) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 **Price** 475 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **2nd** [_protector tree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=976) (resembles giant mushroom, not a tree), [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325) (fungus only), [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=342) (giant mushroom only)
  • **3rd** [_protector tree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=976) (resembles giant mushroom, not a tree), [_stinking cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=309), [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325) (fungus only), [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=366) (resembles wall of fungus; deals poison damage)
Spore Shepherd's Staff (Major) **Source** [Pathfinder #193: Mantle of Gold](/Sources.aspx?ID=206) pg. 85 **Price** 100 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **4th** [_protector tree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=976) (resembles giant mushroom, not a tree), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) (fungus only), [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325) (fungus only)
  • **5th** [_cloudkill_](/Spells.aspx?ID=42), [_plant form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=223) (resemble giant fungus instead of plant, gain fungus trait instead of plant trait; arboreal type only), [_protector tree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=976) (resembles giant mushroom, not a tree), [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325) (fungus only)
_Nethys Note: The level and price of this are as printed in the source._
name: Spore Shepherd's Staff

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>

---

This handsome tassel comes in a variety of colors to match your companion's collar or bridle. While attached, the tassel gives the companion a +1 item bonus to Acrobatics checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=28), and to their Reflex DC against Trip attacks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb **Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 78 **Usage** incorporated into armor **Bulk** L --- Made from preserved crimson worm hide, this thick leather cape can deflect incoming blows without serious damage. It functions as a [dueling cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=680) and while moved into a protective position, it also grants you fire resistance equal to twice the relic's number of gifts.
**Forming the Bond** While wearing the cape, you defeat a foe and spare their life.
**Advancing the Bond** Befriend beasts and terrify foes with your physical presence and prowess.
**Minor Gifts** [feral claws](/Relics.aspx?ID=9), [flare bolt](/Relics.aspx?ID=37), [heat haze](/Relics.aspx?ID=38)
**Major Gifts** [form of fury](/Relics.aspx?ID=10) (snake form), [searing wave](/Relics.aspx?ID=41)
name: Avernal Cape

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb **Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 78 **Bulk** 1 --- This grimoire is constructed from individual tarnished silver plates, bound together to form a fine book. Each “page” contain engraved maps, accounts of sites in Nar-Voth, and daily journals. Throughout the journal, spells and notes are inscribed on the margins and within the text. When Chapter One is completed, this grimoire contains the following spells: [_allfood_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1425), [_deep sight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1426), [_fate's travels_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1427), [_glowing trail_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1428), [_know location_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1429), [_stonesense_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1430), [_wanderer's guide_](/Spells.aspx?ID=368). If you use the grimoire during your daily preparations and are capable of preparing spells of the appropriate level, when you cast a non-damaging spell prepared from this grimoire, you can Step or Stride up to half your Speed after Casting the Spell. To scribe additional spells into the grimoire, you must provide additional silver plates at the cost of 10 gp per spell when Learning the Spell. **Forming the Bond** The PCs locate and bind together the silver plates for Chapter One.
**Advancing the Bond** Locate additional chapters of the journal and bind them together.
**Minor Gifts** [ease burden](/Relics.aspx?ID=25), [word of faith](/Relics.aspx?ID=15)
**Major Gifts** [underground bounty](/Relics.aspx?ID=29)
name: Kalmaug's Journal

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

---

This flask is full of shrapnel and anti-coagulants designed to make targets bleed out. It deals 1 slashing damage, the listed [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and the listed slashing splash damage. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs=""
>
[Everywhen Map](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2670)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 79

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The map changes based on the creature who invests it, showing every path the creature has ever traveled in their lifetime. The map doesn't distinguish between current or past locations. It can be tricked into revealing the path of a different creature if a piece of that creature (such as hair, bone, or skin) is used with a successful DC 24 [Trick Magic Item](/Feats.aspx?ID=857) check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Everywhen Map

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Festrem Mortu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2671)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 79

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The book's cover is a patchwork of tanned [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) skin that reeks of rotting meat. The longer a living creature studies this book, the more the scent of decay appeals to them.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your last action was to cast a necromancy spell prepared from this grimoire;  **Effect** You draw negative energy from the triggering spell. You gain 10 temporary hit points that last for 1 hour.

---

 **Special** If this grimoire is used to prepare spells five days in succession by the same caster, that caster is subjected to the [curse of the ravenous](/Curses.aspx?ID=19).

</column>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 79

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small box contains a clockwork mechanism that rhythmically thumps the ground, allowing creatures with [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) to detect it at up to double their usual range. The mechanism can be wound to thump anywhere from 1 round to 1 minute before falling into useless components.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thumper Snare

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs=""
>
[Demon's Knot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2674)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn necklace

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) Kaivirris is housed in the magnificent ruby that serves as the centerpiece of this beautiful, gold-plated, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) pendant necklace. Creatures within 30 feet of the _demon's knot_ feel a subtle urge to acquire the necklace and take a –1 status penalty to Will saves.

 Upon donning the _demon's knot_, it fuses to you. You are subject to a [_geas_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) that requires you to free Kaivirris from his prison. If you die without completing this task, you become a [sulfur zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1377) that always remains within 100 feet of the _demon's knot_ and is under the control of whoever is cursed by the _demon's knot_.

 The demon's knot sustains your physical form and protects your mind from mental control. You don't need to eat or drink. You become immune to the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) and [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) conditions.

 The _demon's knot_ corrupts anyone wearing it. You gain weakness to cold iron 10 and good 10. Whenever you're within 60 feet of a [celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), you're [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) and can't reduce the value of the frightened condition below 2.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You allow Kaivirris's corrupting influence over you to outwardly manifest. For 1 minute, your features adopt a cruel cast, and you radiate a palpable aura of evil. During this time, you gain a +2 status bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks, and your Strikes deal an additional 1d6 evil damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The demon's knot casts [_chilling darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=36).

**Destruction** A celestial creature must hold the necklace in their hands for at least an hour while meditating on the damage it has caused, then weep on the ruby. It must then be completely sealed within a cold iron container and cast into the heart of an active volcano, allowing the magma to melt the container and the necklace within.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Demon's Knot

    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Terror Spores](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2675)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Inhaled" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=96" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 79

**Price** 110 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Better known as the shrieking toadstool, the tragzioma fungus releases spores when disturbed by browsers, sending nearby creatures into a screaming panic that attracts opportunistic carnivores. Delicate alchemical processes have stabilized and concentrated the spores into a poison.

 **Saving Throw** DC 28 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 round; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (1 round); **Stage 2** frightened 3 (1 round); **Stage 3** frightened 3 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for 1 round (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Terror Spores

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Toxic Effluence](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2676)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Contact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=37" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 80

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dark green sludge has a caustic scent and gritty texture. Capable of entering the body through contact with flesh, toxic effluence becomes harmful once it hits the bloodstream, where it causes internal chemical burns, intense pain, and muscle spasms.

 **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage, 3d6 fire damage, [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison damage, 4d6 fire damage, clumsy 2, and enfeebled 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 5d6 poison damage, 5d6 fire damage, clumsy 3, and enfeebled 3 (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Toxic Effluence

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Volcanic Vigor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2677)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 80

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattoo

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo takes the shape of a muscular [orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=19), with molten flesh and hollow eye sockets that seep smoke. You don't take damage from [extreme heat or severe heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=642). 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You stomp on the ground, causing a 5-foot radius, 30- foot tall cylinder of lava to erupt from the ground within a range of 30 feet. The cylinder deals 7d6 fire damage. All creatures in the area must attempt a DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Volcanic Vigor
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 80

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Limited"
>
Volcanic Vigor (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 80

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The damage of your lava eruption increases to 12d6, the range increases to 60 feet, and you can choose to increase the radius of the cylinder to 10 feet. Similarly, the height of the cylinder increases to 60 feet, and the DC increases to 34. While exposed to incredible heat, you take only minor heat damage every hour, instead of moderate heat damage every minute. You can see through ash and smoke.
name: Volcanic Vigor
